again?<|19.32|> <|19.32|> How is it relevant in this moment in time?<|24.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The Good Samaritan is so familiar to us that even within secular culture, it's a trope that is easily understood and identified.<|11.24|> <|12.30|> Everything from Van Gogh to Samuel L. Jackson, this parable has inspired paintings, sculpture, satire, poetry, photography, and film.<|24.48|> <|24.48|> Thank you.<|25.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The phrase "Good Samaritan," meaning "to help a stranger in need," has been used to name everything from charities to hospitals.<|11.50|> <|11.50|> We even have Good Samaritan laws on the books in every state in the nation to protect those who might otherwise be reluctant to help someone in need.<|25.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> There is so much that is familiar and so much that is timeless in this parable.<|7.60|> <|8.24|> It's truly rich.<|9.62|> <|11.28|> How incredibly timely it would be to preach about loving our neighbor<|17.44|> <|17.44|> in relation to the ICE raids and detention center conditions at our border.<|24.58|> <|24.58|> Amen.<|25.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> How incredibly relevant it would be to preach about the people we pass by every single day,<|7.78|> <|8.54|> some on the very grounds of this cathedral, who are homeless and in need.<|13.90|> <|15.14|> Sadly, there is no shortage of examples.<|20.00|> <|20.00|> And tragically, there are injured and marginalized people all around us,<|27.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> This is a fact, and they are all our neighbors.<|5.98|> <|7.42|> I'm grateful that as a national church and that we as a local parish do a great deal to humanize and aid our brothers and sisters in need.<|19.98|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.00|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But this is where I'm going to take a little bit of a departure.<|3.38|> <|4.38|> One that will point to why I had such a hard time getting a sermon on paper this week.<|11.34|> <|12.52|> And I'm going to ask your forgiveness if this sounds a bit crass at the beginning.<|18.48|> <|19.20|> But in some ways...<|21.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> if we're really honest with ourselves,<|2.68|> <|3.40|> I think it's relatively easy to think about helping these others.<|10.16|> <|10.74|> To proclaim that, yes, they too are our neighbors.<|15.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We give money, we give time, we give prayers to marginalized groups, and I am so glad we do.<|8.26|> <|8.48|> Please don't get me wrong.<|10.50|> <|11.52|> It is our baptismal covenant to uphold the dignity of every human being, to in effect be the Good Samaritan,<|22.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We rally, we volunteer, we lobby, we donate, and then most of us go home.<|7.62|> <|8.52|> Home to a warm bed and the TV news.<|12.18|> <|13.36|> Home to a different kind of neighbor.<|17.38|> <|18.60|> Home.<|19.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I try really hard to pay attention when things keep coming up.<|7.64|> <|7.64|> I happen to be of the belief that the Spirit works that way if we're paying attention.<|13.84|> <|15.04|> And I've had no fewer than five interactions with parishioners in the past two weeks<|21.62|> <|21.62|> where they have had something heavy weighing on their hearts.<|26.44|> <|26.44|> I've had no fewer than five interactions with parishioners in the past two weeks where they have had <|9.12|> All were deeply burdened by their feeling of being hard-hearted.<|14.52|> <|15.54|> And they struggled to forgive all sorts of folks.<|19.24|> <|19.78|> A sibling, a friend, a spouse.<|21.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> politicians, and adherents to certain political agendas.<|6.14|> <|7.56|> All felt that they were falling short and it heavily weighed on them.<|12.88|> <|14.00|> So all week I've been wrestling with this.<|16.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> forgiveness kept emerging.<|3.22|> <|4.12|> And how did that oft-repeated conversation<|7.10|> <|7.10|> and that heart-heavy burden of forgiveness<|11.50|> <|11.50|> tie into the Good Samaritan?<|13.66|> <|15.12|> I think the parishioner who said they struggled<|18.06|> <|18.06|> with the politics of our country<|20.76|> <|20.76|> is what has stayed with me<|23.28|> <|23.28|> and made me certain<|25.18|> <|25.18|> that there is a deep connection.<|28.78|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> They felt an incredible anger and helplessness, a feeling foreign to them, toward our politicians<|10.46|> <|10.46|> and particularly our political climate.<|14.68|> <|14.68|> And that feeling was bleeding into feelings for folks who support certain ideologies.<|23.82|> <|23.82|> This was where their deep sense of worry came in.<|28.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> These people are my neighbors, actual neighbors, and they seem so foreign to me in their beliefs and their support.<|10.88|> <|11.88|> How can I be civil? How can I pray for them?<|15.62|> <|16.62|> Or asked in the context of today's parable, how can I show them mercy?<|23.56|> <|24.18|> How can I be civil?<|24.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Jews and Samaritans shared historical roots, but their respective cultures and religious convictions had diverged over time. Sound familiar?<|15.70|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.66|> Conflict was not uncommon.<|3.02|> <|4.06|> And if you recall that gospel reading just a few weeks ago,<|7.82|> <|8.66|> a Samaritan village refused to offer hospitality to Jesus.<|13.54|> <|13.84|> And James and John, those sons of thunder, wanted to respond with a heavenly firebomb.<|22.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The impulse to destroy is not new, particularly in times of fear.<|8.38|> <|9.00|> And today, in this day in 2019, many on both ends of the political spectrum live in fear.<|20.68|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.00|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And this is why I made my earlier assertion that sometimes it is easier to reach out and help those who are very clearly different than we are.<|14.62|> <|15.62|> It's more obvious what to do.<|18.22|> <|18.74|> And it can make us feel better.<|21.00|> <|21.00|> And at the end of the day, we can retreat back into our comfort.<|28.06|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> How much harder is it to extend love of neighbor to our actual neighbor, to people who look a lot like we do, move through the same spaces we do?<|19.50|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> who have access to all of the same sources for news and information that we do,<|6.08|> <|6.48|> and yet somehow, mysteriously, evaluate it so differently.<|13.10|> <|14.80|> We are only a few clicks of remote control apart, from Fox News to CNN,<|23.58|> <|23.58|> and yet that distance is so vast.<|28.38|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Family at the holiday dinner table, neighbors across the street, folks sitting next to us<|8.76|> <|8.76|> in these pews.<|11.60|> <|11.60|> If you thought Thanksgiving 2016 was difficult, brace yourselves.<|17.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We are embroiled in challenging times, my friends.<|5.80|> <|6.54|> Times where friends and family are and will struggle to be civil, to show mercy.<|15.50|> <|16.78|> Personally, I would advocate staying off of Facebook for the next 18 months,<|21.68|> <|21.68|> but I would like to say,<|23.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> How will you endeavor to love your neighbor?<|5.36|> <|6.16|> Not the stranger lying in the ditch,<|8.84|> <|9.28|> but the actual neighbor who wheels their trash can out to the curb next to yours.<|14.82|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.28|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I think the first part of the gospel lesson today provides some clue.<|6.36|> <|7.94|> This lawyer is often interpreted as adversarial.<|12.04|> <|12.50|> I think it's because there's that word in there, test.<|14.86|> <|15.52|> Who would dare to test Jesus?<|17.98|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But if you read closely, this is what civil discourse looks like.<|7.50|> <|7.50|> A conversation back and forth, respectful engagement of the other.<|16.00|> <|16.00|> Questions, answers, more questions.<|20.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The lawyer asked Jesus, "And who is my neighbor?"<|7.00|> <|7.00|> And Jesus told the parable of the Good Samaritan.<|11.00|> <|11.00|> At the end, Jesus asked the lawyer, "Which of these three do you think was a neighbor to the man?"<|20.00|> <|20.00|> Notice he didn't tell him. He asked him what he thought.<|24.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And the lawyer answered,<|1.86|> <|2.70|> The one who showed him mercy.<|5.30|> <|6.54|> Jesus said to him,<|8.24|> <|9.04|> Go and do likewise.<|11.48|> <|13.44|> Pray for your neighbor,<|14.96|> <|16.24|> whoever they may be,<|17.62|> <|18.30|> whatever they may believe.<|20.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Pray for your own hard heart to soften.<|5.24|> <|6.66|> That is difficult and intentional work, my friends.<|10.08|> <|11.12|> And I will tell you that spending time with dogs can help.<|14.44|> <|17.68|> And as for showing mercy, go and do likewise.<|24.42|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> advice.<|1.66|><|endoftext|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The lawyer asked Jesus, "And who is my neighbor?"<|7.00|>
<|7.00|> And Jesus told the parable of the Good Samaritan.<|11.00|>
<|11.00|> At the end, Jesus asked the lawyer, "Which of these three do you think was a neighbor to the man?"<|20.00|>
<|20.00|> Notice he didn't tell him. He asked him what he thought.<|24.00|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And the lawyer answered,<|1.86|>
<|2.70|> The one who showed him mercy.<|5.30|>
<|6.54|> Jesus said to him,<|8.24|>
<|9.04|> Go and do likewise.<|11.48|>
<|13.44|> Pray for your neighbor,<|14.96|>
<|16.24|> whoever they may be,<|17.62|>
<|18.30|> whatever they may believe.<|20.40|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Pray for your own hard heart to soften.<|5.24|>
<|6.66|> That is difficult and intentional work, my friends.<|10.08|>
<|11.12|> And I will tell you that spending time with dogs can help.<|14.44|>
<|17.68|> And as for showing mercy, go and do likewise.<|24.42|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> advice.<|1.66|><|endoftext|>
